<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shiozuka Kofun]][[en:Shiozuka Kofun]][[ja:塩塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shiozuka Kofun]][[en:Shiozuka Kofun]][[ja:塩塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shiozuka Kofun]][[en:Shiozuka Kofun]][[ja:塩塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11428650]] Wikidata name: Shiozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiozuka Kofun | native_name =塩塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shiozuka-kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shiozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.70302778|N|135.79283333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|10.9|N|135|47|34.2|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shiozuka Kofun'''|塩塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located on the border of the Utahime-chō and Saki-chō neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2025|title=塩塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shiozaki Kofun is located on the Saki Hills, which mark the northern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It is on the eastern edge of the western group of the Saki Tatenami Kofun cluster, separated from the Saki Misasagiyama Kofun and Saki Ishizukayama Kofun, which are in the western group, by a valley that runs north-to-south. There are many medium-sized tumuli in the Saki Tatenami Tomb Group, including the Emontomaruzuka Kofun (45m), Oseyama Kofun (50m), [[:en:Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun]] (96m), and Nekozuka Kofun (110m), in the vicinity. The tumulus was a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but in 1956 was partially leveled for use as an orchard. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] were conducted by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture, focusing on the posterior circular mound. In 1973, there was a plan to level the mound and develop it as well, but thanks to the efforts of the Nara Prefectural Board of Education, the landowner agreed to preserve it, and in 1975 it was designated a national historic site.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus measured 105 meters in length, with the posterior circular portion 70 meters in diameter and nine meters high. The anterior rectangular portion was 55 meters in width at the front and 1.5 meters high. The anterior portion is unusually low and flat compared to the circular portion, and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] have been excavated, so it is possible that this portion of the tumulus was leveled and a building was constructed there as far back as the Nara period. Maps also indicate that during the Nara period the area was a pine forest garden in the vicinity of [[Heijō-kyō]] Palace, and it is thought that the tumulus was incorporated into that site. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures or ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found in the investigation of the tumulus, but trench investigations suggest that the mound was built in two levels. There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The 1956 excavation uncovered a clay coffin with its main axis facing north-and-south. The center of the clay coffin was lost due to [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]], but both ends remain, confirming that had a length of 6.80 meters, width of 1.45 meters in the north tapering to 1.30 meters wide in the south. Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been lost due to grave robbery, but two iron swords, one iron knife (remnants), four "warabite"-style knives, 15 iron axes, and 9 iron sickles were excavated. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|'''Shiozuka Kofun'''|塩塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located on the border of the Utahime-chō and Saki-chō neighborhoods of the city of [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2025|title=塩塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shiozaki Kofun is located on the Saki Hills, which mark the northern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It is on the eastern edge of the western group of the Saki Tatenami Kofun cluster, separated from the Saki Misasagiyama Kofun and Saki Ishizukayama Kofun, which are in the western group, by a valley that runs north-to-south. There are many medium-sized tumuli in the Saki Tatenami Tomb Group, including the Emontomaruzuka Kofun (45m), Oseyama Kofun (50m), [[:en:Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun]] (96m), and Nekozuka Kofun (110m), in the vicinity. The tumulus was a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but in 1956 was partially leveled for use as an orchard. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] were conducted by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture, focusing on the posterior circular mound. In 1973, there was a plan to level the mound and develop it as well, but thanks to the efforts of the Nara Prefectural Board of Education, the landowner agreed to preserve it, and in 1975 it was designated a national historic site.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus measured 105 meters in length, with the posterior circular portion 70 meters in diameter and nine meters high. The anterior rectangular portion was 55 meters in width at the front and 1.5 meters high. The anterior portion is unusually low and flat compared to the circular portion, and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] have been excavated, so it is possible that this portion of the tumulus was leveled and a building was constructed there as far back as the Nara period. Maps also indicate that during the Nara period the area was a pine forest garden in the vicinity of [[Heijō-kyō]] Palace, and it is thought that the tumulus was incorporated into that site. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures or ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found in the investigation of the tumulus, but trench investigations suggest that the mound was built in two levels. There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The 1956 excavation uncovered a clay coffin with its main axis facing north-and-south. The center of the clay coffin was lost due to [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]], but both ends remain, confirming that had a length of 6.80 meters, width of 1.45 meters in the north tapering to 1.30 meters wide in the south. Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been lost due to grave robbery, but two iron swords, one iron knife (remnants), four "warabite"-style knives, 15 iron axes, and 9 iron sickles were excavated. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2025|title=塩塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shiozaki Kofun is located on the Saki Hills, which mark the northern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. It is on the eastern edge of the western group of the Saki Tatenami Kofun cluster, separated from the Saki Misasagiyama Kofun and Saki Ishizukayama Kofun, which are in the western group, by a valley that runs north-to-south. There are many medium-sized tumuli in the Saki Tatenami Tomb Group, including the Emontomaruzuka Kofun (45m), Oseyama Kofun (50m), [[:en:Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun]] (96m), and Nekozuka Kofun (110m), in the vicinity. The tumulus was a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but in 1956 was partially leveled for use as an orchard. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] were conducted by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture, focusing on the posterior circular mound. In 1973, there was a plan to level the mound and develop it as well, but thanks to the efforts of the Nara Prefectural Board of Education, the landowner agreed to preserve it, and in 1975 it was designated a national historic site.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus measured 105 meters in length, with the posterior circular portion 70 meters in diameter and nine meters high. The anterior rectangular portion was 55 meters in width at the front and 1.5 meters high. The anterior portion is unusually low and flat compared to the circular portion, and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] have been excavated, so it is possible that this portion of the tumulus was leveled and a building was constructed there as far back as the Nara period. Maps also indicate that during the Nara period the area was a pine forest garden in the vicinity of [[Heijō-kyō]] Palace, and it is thought that the tumulus was incorporated into that site. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' clay figures or ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found in the investigation of the tumulus, but trench investigations suggest that the mound was built in two levels. There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The 1956 excavation uncovered a clay coffin with its main axis facing north-and-south. The center of the clay coffin was lost due to [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]], but both ends remain, confirming that had a length of 6.80 meters, width of 1.45 meters in the north tapering to 1.30 meters wide in the south. Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. Most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] had been lost due to grave robbery, but two iron swords, one iron knife (remnants), four "warabite"-style knives, 15 iron axes, and 9 iron sickles were excavated. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Heijō Station|Heijō Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

In 1973, there was a plan to level the mound and develop it as well, but thanks to the efforts of the Nara Prefectural Board of Education, the landowner agreed to preserve it, and in 1975 it was designated a national historic site.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus measured 105 meters in length, with the posterior circular portion 70 meters in diameter and nine meters high. The anterior rectangular portion was 55 meters in width at the front and 1.5 meters high. The anterior portion is unusually low and flat compared to the circular portion, and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} have been excavated, so it is possible that this portion of the tumulus was leveled and a building was constructed there as far back as the Nara period. Maps also indicate that during the Nara period the area was a pine forest garden in the vicinity of [[Heijō-kyō]] Palace, and it is thought that the tumulus was incorporated into that site. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures or ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found in the investigation of the tumulus, but trench investigations suggest that the mound was built in two levels. There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The 1956 excavation uncovered a clay coffin with its main axis facing north-and-south. The center of the clay coffin was lost due to {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery}}, but both ends remain, confirming that had a length of 6.80 meters, width of 1.45 meters in the north tapering to 1.30 meters wide in the south. Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. Most of the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} had been lost due to grave robbery, but two iron swords, one iron knife (remnants), four "warabite"-style knives, 15 iron axes, and 9 iron sickles were excavated. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Shiozuka Kofun'''|塩塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located on the border of the Utahime-chō and Saki-chō neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2025|title=塩塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shiozaki Kofun is located on the Saki Hills, which mark the northern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. It is on the eastern edge of the western group of the Saki Tatenami Kofun cluster, separated from the Saki Misasagiyama Kofun and Saki Ishizukayama Kofun, which are in the western group, by a valley that runs north-to-south. There are many medium-sized tumuli in the Saki Tatenami Tomb Group, including the Emontomaruzuka Kofun (45m), Oseyama Kofun (50m), {{ill|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|en|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|WD=Q11383488|lt=Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun}} (96m), and Nekozuka Kofun (110m), in the vicinity. The tumulus was a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but in 1956 was partially leveled for use as an orchard. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] were conducted by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture, focusing on the posterior circular mound. In 1973, there was a plan to level the mound and develop it as well, but thanks to the efforts of the Nara Prefectural Board of Education, the landowner agreed to preserve it, and in 1975 it was designated a national historic site.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus measured 105 meters in length, with the posterior circular portion 70 meters in diameter and nine meters high. The anterior rectangular portion was 55 meters in width at the front and 1.5 meters high. The anterior portion is unusually low and flat compared to the circular portion, and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} have been excavated, so it is possible that this portion of the tumulus was leveled and a building was constructed there as far back as the Nara period. Maps also indicate that during the Nara period the area was a pine forest garden in the vicinity of [[Heijō-kyō]] Palace, and it is thought that the tumulus was incorporated into that site. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures or ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found in the investigation of the tumulus, but trench investigations suggest that the mound was built in two levels. There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The center of the clay coffin was lost due to {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery}}, but both ends remain, confirming that had a length of 6.80 meters, width of 1.45 meters in the north tapering to 1.30 meters wide in the south. Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. Most of the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} had been lost due to grave robbery, but two iron swords, one iron knife (remnants), four "warabite"-style knives, 15 iron axes, and 9 iron sickles were excavated. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiozuka Kofun | native_name =塩塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shiozuka-kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shiozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.70302778|N|135.79283333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|10.9|N|135|47|34.2|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shiozuka Kofun'''|塩塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located on the border of the Utahime-chō and Saki-chō neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2025|title=塩塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shiozaki Kofun is located on the Saki Hills, which mark the northern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. It is on the eastern edge of the western group of the Saki Tatenami Kofun cluster, separated from the Saki Misasagiyama Kofun and Saki Ishizukayama Kofun, which are in the western group, by a valley that runs north-to-south. There are many medium-sized tumuli in the Saki Tatenami Tomb Group, including the Emontomaruzuka Kofun (45m), Oseyama Kofun (50m), {{ill|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|en|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|WD=Q11383488|lt=Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun}} (96m), and Nekozuka Kofun (110m), in the vicinity. The tumulus was a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but in 1956 was partially leveled for use as an orchard. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] were conducted by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture, focusing on the posterior circular mound. In 1973, there was a plan to level the mound and develop it as well, but thanks to the efforts of the Nara Prefectural Board of Education, the landowner agreed to preserve it, and in 1975 it was designated a national historic site.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus measured 105 meters in length, with the posterior circular portion 70 meters in diameter and nine meters high. The anterior rectangular portion was 55 meters in width at the front and 1.5 meters high. The anterior portion is unusually low and flat compared to the circular portion, and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} have been excavated, so it is possible that this portion of the tumulus was leveled and a building was constructed there as far back as the Nara period. Maps also indicate that during the Nara period the area was a pine forest garden in the vicinity of [[Heijō-kyō]] Palace, and it is thought that the tumulus was incorporated into that site. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' clay figures or ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found in the investigation of the tumulus, but trench investigations suggest that the mound was built in two levels. There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The 1956 excavation uncovered a clay coffin with its main axis facing north-and-south. The center of the clay coffin was lost due to {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery}}, but both ends remain, confirming that had a length of 6.80 meters, width of 1.45 meters in the north tapering to 1.30 meters wide in the south. Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. Most of the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} had been lost due to grave robbery, but two iron swords, one iron knife (remnants), four "warabite"-style knives, 15 iron axes, and 9 iron sickles were excavated. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiozuka Kofun | native_name =塩塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shiozuka-kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shiozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.70302778|N|135.79283333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|10.9|N|135|47|34.2|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shiozuka Kofun'''|塩塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located on the border of the Utahime-chō and Saki-chō neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2025|title=塩塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shiozaki Kofun is located on the Saki Hills, which mark the northern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. It is on the eastern edge of the western group of the Saki Tatenami Kofun cluster, separated from the Saki Misasagiyama Kofun and Saki Ishizukayama Kofun, which are in the western group, by a valley that runs north-to-south. There are many medium-sized tumuli in the Saki Tatenami Tomb Group, including the Emontomaruzuka Kofun (45m), Oseyama Kofun (50m), {{ill|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|en|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|WD=Q11383488|lt=Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|1=Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun}} (96m), and Nekozuka Kofun (110m), in the vicinity. The tumulus was a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but in 1956 was partially leveled for use as an orchard. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] were conducted by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture, focusing on the posterior circular mound. In 1973, there was a plan to level the mound and develop it as well, but thanks to the efforts of the Nara Prefectural Board of Education, the landowner agreed to preserve it, and in 1975 it was designated a national historic site.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus measured 105 meters in length, with the posterior circular portion 70 meters in diameter and nine meters high. The anterior rectangular portion was 55 meters in width at the front and 1.5 meters high. The anterior portion is unusually low and flat compared to the circular portion, and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} have been excavated, so it is possible that this portion of the tumulus was leveled and a building was constructed there as far back as the Nara period. Maps also indicate that during the Nara period the area was a pine forest garden in the vicinity of [[Heijō-kyō]] Palace, and it is thought that the tumulus was incorporated into that site. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures or ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found in the investigation of the tumulus, but trench investigations suggest that the mound was built in two levels. There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The 1956 excavation uncovered a clay coffin with its main axis facing north-and-south. The center of the clay coffin was lost due to {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery|1=Grave robbery}}, but both ends remain, confirming that had a length of 6.80 meters, width of 1.45 meters in the north tapering to 1.30 meters wide in the south. Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. Most of the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} had been lost due to grave robbery, but two iron swords, one iron knife (remnants), four "warabite"-style knives, 15 iron axes, and 9 iron sickles were excavated. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station|1=Heijō Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiozuka Kofun]][[en:Shiozuka Kofun]][[ja:塩塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shiozuka Kofun]][[en:Shiozuka Kofun]][[ja:塩塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shiozuka Kofun]][[ja:塩塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Shiozuka Kofun | native_name =塩塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shiozuka-kofun.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shiozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shiozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.70302778|N|135.79283333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of ={{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|10.9|N|135|47|34.2|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Shiozuka Kofun'''|塩塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun|Kofun]], located on the border of the Utahime-chō and Saki-chō neighborhoods of the city of {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1975.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2025|title=塩塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Shiozaki Kofun is located on the Saki Hills, which mark the northern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. It is on the eastern edge of the western group of the Saki Tatenami Kofun cluster, separated from the Saki Misasagiyama Kofun and Saki Ishizukayama Kofun, which are in the western group, by a valley that runs north-to-south. There are many medium-sized tumuli in the Saki Tatenami Tomb Group, including the Emontomaruzuka Kofun (45m), Oseyama Kofun (50m), {{ill|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|en|Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|WD=Q11383488|lt=Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun|1=Saki Hyōtanyama Kofun}} (96m), and Nekozuka Kofun (110m), in the vicinity. The tumulus was a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, but in 1956 was partially leveled for use as an orchard. [[:en:Archaeological excavations|Archaeological excavations]] were conducted by the Kashihara Archaeological Institute of Nara Prefecture, focusing on the posterior circular mound. In 1973, there was a plan to level the mound and develop it as well, but thanks to the efforts of the Nara Prefectural Board of Education, the landowner agreed to preserve it, and in 1975 it was designated a national historic site.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus measured 105 meters in length, with the posterior circular portion 70 meters in diameter and nine meters high. The anterior rectangular portion was 55 meters in width at the front and 1.5 meters high. The anterior portion is unusually low and flat compared to the circular portion, and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} have been excavated, so it is possible that this portion of the tumulus was leveled and a building was constructed there as far back as the Nara period. Maps also indicate that during the Nara period the area was a pine forest garden in the vicinity of [[Heijō-kyō]] Palace, and it is thought that the tumulus was incorporated into that site. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures or ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found in the investigation of the tumulus, but trench investigations suggest that the mound was built in two levels. There is trace of a moat around the anterior portion, but a horseshoe-shaped ditch exists around the posterior portion, although there is no sign of it having contained water.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The 1956 excavation uncovered a clay coffin with its main axis facing north-and-south. The center of the clay coffin was lost due to {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery|1=Grave robbery}}, but both ends remain, confirming that had a length of 6.80 meters, width of 1.45 meters in the north tapering to 1.30 meters wide in the south. Traces of a wooden coffin were found inside the clay coffin. Most of the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} had been lost due to grave robbery, but two iron swords, one iron knife (remnants), four "warabite"-style knives, 15 iron axes, and 9 iron sickles were excavated. This clay coffin was found slightly east of the centerline of the circular mound, indicating that there may be another burial facility located parallel to it.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Heijō Station|en|Heijō Station|WD=Q5699170|lt=Heijō Station|1=Heijō Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Kyoto Line|Kintetsu Kyoto Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Shiozuka Kofun}} *{{webarchive|title=Overview of the Saki Shield Row Tombs |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150828032742/http://beauty.geocities.jp/belltechnique/gaiyou/sub3.html}} (Saki no Kai, a volunteer tourist guide group){{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0364.html Nara Prefecture home page]{{in lang|ja}}
